cards.back: A group that receives the standard or control treatment, used as a baseline for comparison
A variable that is intentionally changed by the researcher to observe its effect on the dependent variable
A variable being measured or observed in response to changes made to the independent variable
A process of assigning participants or observations to treatment groups randomly to minimize bias and ensure representation
The phenomenon where participants experience a perceived benefit from a treatment or intervention due to their expectations, rather than any actual effect
